scaled_oracle_cases.description: Scaled TMFA problem with expanded model (5 reactions, 3 compounds), flux lower bound clamping for irreversible reactions, sorting, and verification checks.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function handles a larger model with sorted reactions and compounds, clamps negative flux lower bounds to zero, and passes verification checks.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must process all reactions and compounds in alphabetical order; for reactions with negative lower bound, set it to 0; reactions must have bounds defined; the output should include all variables and constraints correctly.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Without sorting, the variable keys may appear in insertion order. Without clamping, negative bounds persist. Without missing bounds check, the function may fail with KeyError instead of ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
prob, v, zi, dgri, xij, compounds = make_tmfa_problem(model, solver)
print("Namespaces:", list(prob.namespaces.keys()))
print("Flux vars:", v.vars)
print("Binary vars:", zi.vars)
print("Gibbs vars:", dgri.vars)
print("Concentration vars:", xij.vars)
print("Compounds:", compounds)
result = {'prob': prob, 'v': v, 'zi': zi, 'dgri': dgri, 'xij': xij, 'compounds': compounds}
scaled_oracle_cases.assertion_code:
assert len(v.vars) == 5
assert len(zi.vars) == 5
assert len(dgri.vars) == 5
assert len(xij.vars) == 3
assert len(prob.constraints) == 3
# Check clamping
assert v.vars[('R1',)]['lower'] == 0
assert v.vars[('R3',)]['lower'] == 0
assert v.vars[('R5',)]['lower'] == 0
assert v.vars[('R2',)]['lower'] == 0
assert v.vars[('R4',)]['lower'] == 10
# Check that keys are in alphabetical order
assert list(v.vars.keys()) == [('R1',), ('R2',), ('R3',), ('R4',), ('R5',)]
assert [k[0] for k in zi.vars.keys()] == ['R1','R2','R3','R4','R5']
assert [k[0] for k in dgri.vars.keys()] == ['R1','R2','R3','R4','R5']
assert [k[0] for k in xij.vars.keys()] == ['A','B','C']
covered_requirements: Increase data complexity by enlarging the model and adding extra attributes that the function should ignore.
Add constraint that irreversible reactions must have non-negative lower bound for flux variable.
Add multiple executable test cases covering normal, edge, and adversarial scenarios to strengthen verification.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test that the function raises ValueError when a reaction is missing bounds.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function raises ValueError if any reaction lacks bounds in mm_irreversible.limits.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must raise ValueError when a reaction in mm_irreversible.reactions is not present in mm_irreversible.limits.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Without the check, the function may proceed with KeyError or use a default value, violating the robust contract.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
model = MetabolicModel()
del model.limits['R1']
solver = Solver('glpk')
covered_requirements: Ensure the function raises an error when a reaction is missing bounds.
